entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The primary crop used in the farming systems of the Sahel region

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of crop yields from year to year

entities.name: YIELD DISPARITY
entities.description: The difference in crop yields between different households in a village

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total biological mass of the pearl millet crop

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area of pearl millet

entities.name: APSIM
entities.description: A crop simulation model used to estimate biomass and grain yields

entities.name: GIS
entities.description: A geographic information system used to combine simulated yields with land tenure maps

entities.name: LINEAR REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze the relationship between field dispersion and yield variability

entities.name: RAINFALL INTERPOLATION
entities.description: A method used to estimate daily rainfall at a high spatial resolution

entities.name: AGRO-CLIMATIC RISK
entities.description: The risk posed by erratic rainfall patterns and spatial variability in the Sahel

entities.name: FERTILITY GRADIENT
entities.description: The variation in soil fertility levels around a village

entities.name: FIELD DISPERSION
entities.description: A farmer strategy involving the scattering of fields to mitigate climatic risk

entities.name: SPATIAL DISPERSION INDEX
entities.description: A metric used to quantify the degree of scattering of household fields

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: The semi-arid region of Africa where the study was conducted

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country in the Sahel where the research took place

entities.name: FAKARA REGION
entities.description: The specific region in Southwest Niger where rain gauges were placed

entities.name: BANIZOUMBOU VILLAGE
entities.description: The village in the Fakara region where land tenure and yields were assessed
